p1.physical_facts: String of Nickels in yellow pot. Dense foliage cluster, consistent leaf count.
p1.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the sequence. No significant growth or decline observed.
p1.visual_health_inference: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2.physical_facts: Mexican Mint in black pot. Two primary leaves, central position.
p2.explanatory_transformations: Significant decline. The plant has withered and collapsed into the soil substrate over the 5-day period.
p2.visual_health_inference: Critical/Deceased. The plant tissue has lost turgor pressure and shows signs of necrosis.

p3.physical_facts: Pothos in black pot with white rabbit anchor. Two leaves present.
p3.explanatory_transformations: The Pothos has undergone rapid senescence. The leaves have detached or fully withered into the soil.
p3.visual_health_inference: Critical. The plant is no longer viable; the foliage has completely collapsed.

p4.physical_facts: Silver Guest in black pot. Small seedling near the rim.
p4.explanatory_transformations: The seedling has failed to thrive and appears to have desiccated alongside the other occupants of the black pot.
p4.visual_health_inference: Critical. No signs of active growth; likely non-viable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in the black pots has become increasingly dry and compacted over the sequence.
biome_observations.debris: Accumulation of withered plant matter on the surface of the black pots.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a rapid decline in the health of the plants in the black pots (p2, p3, p4) starting from T-2, leading to total collapse by the current state.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic failure of the plants in the black pots, likely due to desiccation or root-zone failure. The yellow pot (p1) remains the only stable element.
vision_report.anomalies: The rapid loss of turgor in p2 and p3 suggests a sudden environmental stressor or lack of hydration.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a severe decline in the black-potted specimens. While the String of Nickels (p1) remains resilient, the Mexican Mint, Pothos, and Silver Guest have succumbed to environmental stress, resulting in total foliage collapse and soil desiccation.
